trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline GRU-DKT pipeline is valid; 5 epochs produced val AUC ~0.7528 and hidden test eval AUC ~0.7527 in under 1 minute including evaluation. Training/validation losses were still declining, suggesting undertraining. Analysis: Time budget permits a longer run, and eval pipeline works. A larger recurrent state may capture more student history patterns on ASSISTments while staying small. Decision: train the existing DKT model for 20 epochs with hidden_dim=512 and moderate dropout, then submit the best validation checkpoint.

trajectory.description: Observation: The 20-epoch hidden_dim=512 GRU improved hidden AUC from 0.7527 to 0.7637; validation best occurred at epoch 13 and curves show train loss continuing down while val loss mostly plateaued, so single-model overfitting starts early. Analysis: A low-risk next improvement is checkpoint ensembling across random seeds/dropout settings; this often improves AUC without changing the validated prediction contract, and the measured runtime leaves enough budget for a multi-seed run. Decision: update train/predict to support ensemble checkpoints, then train three moderately regularized GRU-DKT models and submit the averaged ensemble.

trajectory.description: Observation: The LSTM validation ensemble underperformed the current best GRU train+val ensemble on hidden AUC (0.7638 vs 0.7680), but it is architecturally diverse and already trained. Analysis: A uniform merge would likely dilute the stronger GRU ensemble, but a small weighted contribution from LSTM may reduce variance and improve ranking if its errors are complementary. This is a fast, low-risk evaluation because no retraining is needed and the current best remains available. Decision: update predict.py to support optional per-member weights, create a 90% GRU / 10% LSTM weighted checkpoint, smoke-test, and submit.

trajectory.command: python train_pfa.py --C 0.5 --max_iter 300
trajectory.description: Observation: Current best is a 90/10 GRU/LSTM weighted ensemble at hidden AUC 0.768013, only marginally above the 7-seed GRU ensemble; LSTM alone is weaker. Analysis: Same-architecture neural ensembling is saturated, so a complementary non-neural sequential feature model may add independent ranking signal. A PFA-style logistic model using prior per-student/per-skill attempts, wins, failures, and global skill priors is fast to train and causal at prediction time. Decision: implement a PFA logistic trainer/evaluator and extend predict.py/checkpoint loading to optionally blend PFA probabilities with neural ensemble; first train on train and evaluate val to estimate useful blend weights before using train+val.
